---
title: "IBERIA"
subtitle: "Echocardiographic radiomics for atrial fibrillation recurrence"
description: |
  A high-dimensional radiomics study of post-ablation atrial fibrillation
  recurrence. 851 echocardiographic radiomic features evaluated against
  clinical predictors using stability selection and Bayesian variable
  selection — a rigorously characterised negative result.
categories: [active, radiomics, atrial-fibrillation, echocardiography, Bayesian]
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

IBERIA asks whether echocardiographic radiomic features — quantitative
descriptors of texture, intensity, and shape extracted from standard 2D
echo views — carry prognostic information for atrial fibrillation
recurrence beyond what established clinical predictors already provide.

The answer, after substantial methodological care, is **no**. Across
nested cross-validation, Meinshausen–Bühlmann stability selection, and
Bayesian variable selection with horseshoe priors, no radiomic feature
achieves a posterior inclusion probability or stability frequency
comparable to the routine clinical predictors. This is a *characterised*
negative result — one in which the absence of signal has been distinguished
from the absence of power, signal-to-noise has been quantified via Fisher's
discriminant ratio and Cohen's *d*, and alternative explanations have been
systematically ruled out.

The methodological infrastructure built for IBERIA — the stability-selection
audit, the Bayesian variable-selection pipeline with prior sensitivity
analysis, and the conformal prediction layer — has since been generalised
into the `opsis` R package and is being reused across CardioNIR and DynaCARD.

::: {.callout-note}
Sub-project pages (study design, feature pipeline, stability-selection
audit, Bayesian model card, methodological retrospective) are forthcoming.
:::
